R-Based Forecasting Project: Construction Cost Index (2015=100)

Adın Soyadın - Öğrenci Numarası

2026-05-28

1. Project Overview

This project forecasts the Construction Cost Index (2015=100) published by TurkStat (TÜİK) using monthly time series data accessed directly through the tuikr R package. The forecast target is February 2026, the next period after the latest available observation (January 2026).

2. Data Access via tuikr

library(tuikr)
library(httr)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

tables_price <- statistical_tables(theme = "6")

url_cci <- tables_price$table_url[
  tables_price$table_name == "Construction Cost Index (2015=100) and Rate of Change "]

response_cci <- GET(
  url_cci,
  add_headers(
    "User-Agent" = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36",
    "Accept"     = "application/json, text/plain, */*",
    "Referer"    = "https://veriportali.tuik.gov.tr/"
  )
)

cat("HTTP Status:", status_code(response_cci), "\n")
## HTTP Status: 200
cat("Data access date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d"), "\n")
## Data access date: 2026-05-28
raw_cci <- content(response_cci, as = "raw")
tmp_cci <- tempfile(fileext = ".xls")
writeBin(raw_cci, tmp_cci)
df_cci  <- read_excel(tmp_cci)

3. Data Preparation

colnames(df_cci) <- c("year","Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                       "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec")

df_index <- df_cci[6:17, ]

df_long <- df_index %>%
  mutate(year = as.integer(year)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = Jan:Dec, names_to = "month", values_to = "index") %>%
  mutate(
    month_num = match(month, c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                                "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec")),
    index = as.numeric(index)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(index)) %>%
  arrange(year, month_num)

ts_cci <- ts(df_long$index, start = c(2015, 1), frequency = 12)

cat("Latest available observation: 2026 January\n")
## Latest available observation: 2026 January
cat("Forecast target period: 2026 February\n")
## Forecast target period: 2026 February
cat("Number of observations:", nrow(df_long), "\n")
## Number of observations: 133

4. Exploratory Time Series Analysis

plot_data <- data.frame(
  date  = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  index = as.numeric(ts_cci)
)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = date, y = index)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 1.5) +
  labs(
    title    = "Construction Cost Index (2015=100)",
    subtitle = "Turkey, Monthly (January 2015 - January 2026)",
    x = "Year", y = "Index Value",
    caption  = "Source: TurkStat (TÜİK)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

The series shows a strong upward trend, especially from 2021 onward due to high inflation in Turkey. Seasonality is weak relative to the dominant trend. No missing values detected.

5. Naive Forecasting

naive_forecast <- as.numeric(tail(ts_cci, 1))
naive_fitted   <- c(NA, as.numeric(ts_cci[-length(ts_cci)]))
naive_errors   <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - naive_fitted

naive_bias <- mean(naive_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
naive_mad  <- mean(abs(naive_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
naive_mse  <- mean(naive_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
naive_mape <- mean(abs(naive_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
naive_rsfe <- sum(naive_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
naive_ts   <- naive_rsfe / naive_mad

cat("Naive Forecast Feb 2026:", round(naive_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Naive Forecast Feb 2026: 2210.24
cat("Bias:", round(naive_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(naive_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(naive_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(naive_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 16.01 | MAD: 16.27 | MSE: 1253.79 | MAPE: 2.45 %
cat("RSFE:", round(naive_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(naive_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 2113.11 | Tracking Signal: 129.87
naive_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = naive_fitted
)

ggplot(naive_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Naive Forecast"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Naive Forecast" = "red")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Naive Forecast", x = "Year",
       y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

6. Moving Average

window     <- 3
ma_fitted  <- as.numeric(stats::filter(ts_cci, rep(1/window, window), sides = 1))
ma_forecast <- mean(tail(as.numeric(ts_cci), window))

ma_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - ma_fitted
ma_bias <- mean(ma_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
ma_mad  <- mean(abs(ma_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
ma_mse  <- mean(ma_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
ma_mape <- mean(abs(ma_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
ma_rsfe <- sum(ma_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
ma_ts   <- ma_rsfe / ma_mad

cat("MA(3) Forecast Feb 2026:", round(ma_forecast, 2), "\n")
## MA(3) Forecast Feb 2026: 2070.19
cat("Bias:", round(ma_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(ma_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(ma_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(ma_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 15.62 | MAD: 15.83 | MSE: 889.04 | MAPE: 2.38 %
cat("RSFE:", round(ma_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(ma_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 2046.6 | Tracking Signal: 129.28
ma_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = ma_fitted
)

ggplot(ma_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "MA(3)"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue", "MA(3)" = "darkgreen")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Moving Average (3-month)", x = "Year",
       y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

7. Weighted Moving Average

w <- c(0.1, 0.3, 0.6)
n <- length(ts_cci)
wma_fitted <- rep(NA, n)
for (i in 3:n) {
  wma_fitted[i] <- sum(w * as.numeric(ts_cci)[(i-2):i])
}
wma_forecast <- sum(w * tail(as.numeric(ts_cci), 3))

wma_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - wma_fitted
wma_bias <- mean(wma_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
wma_mad  <- mean(abs(wma_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
wma_mse  <- mean(wma_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
wma_mape <- mean(abs(wma_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
wma_rsfe <- sum(wma_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
wma_ts   <- wma_rsfe / wma_mad

cat("WMA Forecast Feb 2026:", round(wma_forecast, 2), "\n")
## WMA Forecast Feb 2026: 2128.53
cat("Bias:", round(wma_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(wma_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(wma_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(wma_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 7.91 | MAD: 8.02 | MSE: 251.47 | MAPE: 1.2 %
cat("RSFE:", round(wma_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(wma_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 1036.5 | Tracking Signal: 129.18
wma_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = wma_fitted
)

ggplot(wma_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "WMA"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue", "WMA" = "orange")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Weighted Moving Average (0.1, 0.3, 0.6)",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

8. Exponential Smoothing

library(forecast)

# Test alpha = 0.9 (high - suitable for trending series)
es_model <- ses(ts_cci, alpha = 0.9, h = 1)
es_forecast <- as.numeric(es_model$mean)
es_fitted <- as.numeric(es_model$fitted)

es_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - es_fitted
es_bias <- mean(es_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
es_mad  <- mean(abs(es_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
es_mse  <- mean(es_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
es_mape <- mean(abs(es_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
es_rsfe <- sum(es_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
es_ts   <- es_rsfe / es_mad

cat("Exponential Smoothing (alpha=0.9) Forecast Feb 2026:", 
    round(es_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Exponential Smoothing (alpha=0.9) Forecast Feb 2026: 2190.14
cat("Bias:", round(es_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(es_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(es_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(es_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 17.48 | MAD: 17.74 | MSE: 1361.45 | MAPE: 2.67 %
cat("RSFE:", round(es_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(es_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 2325.33 | Tracking Signal: 131.05
es_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = es_fitted
)

ggplot(es_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "ES (alpha=0.9)"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "ES (alpha=0.9)" = "purple")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Exponential Smoothing (alpha=0.9)",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

9. Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt’s Method)

# Holt's method - suitable since series has strong trend
holt_model <- holt(ts_cci, alpha = 0.9, beta = 0.3, h = 1)
holt_forecast <- as.numeric(holt_model$mean)
holt_fitted <- as.numeric(holt_model$fitted)

holt_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - holt_fitted
holt_bias <- mean(holt_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
holt_mad  <- mean(abs(holt_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
holt_mse  <- mean(holt_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
holt_mape <- mean(abs(holt_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
holt_rsfe <- sum(holt_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
holt_ts   <- holt_rsfe / holt_mad

cat("Holt's Forecast Feb 2026:", round(holt_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Holt's Forecast Feb 2026: 2267.56
cat("Bias:", round(holt_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(holt_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(holt_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(holt_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 1.86 | MAD: 12.29 | MSE: 885.62 | MAPE: 1.82 %
cat("RSFE:", round(holt_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(holt_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 247.88 | Tracking Signal: 20.17
holt_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = holt_fitted
)

ggplot(holt_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Holt's Method"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Holt's Method" = "darkred")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt's)",
       subtitle = "alpha=0.9, beta=0.3",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

10. Linear Trend Projection

# Create time index
t <- 1:length(ts_cci)
trend_model <- lm(as.numeric(ts_cci) ~ t)
summary(trend_model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = as.numeric(ts_cci) ~ t)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -470.1 -269.7  -14.2  286.8  700.1 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) -361.5815    53.3240  -6.781  3.7e-10 ***
## t             14.0731     0.6905  20.380  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 305.7 on 131 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.7602, Adjusted R-squared:  0.7584 
## F-statistic: 415.3 on 1 and 131 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
trend_fitted <- as.numeric(fitted(trend_model))
trend_forecast <- predict(trend_model, newdata = data.frame(t = length(ts_cci) + 1))

trend_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - trend_fitted
trend_bias <- mean(trend_errors)
trend_mad  <- mean(abs(trend_errors))
trend_mse  <- mean(trend_errors^2)
trend_mape <- mean(abs(trend_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100)
trend_rsfe <- sum(trend_errors)
trend_ts   <- trend_rsfe / trend_mad

cat("Linear Trend Forecast Feb 2026:", round(trend_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Linear Trend Forecast Feb 2026: 1524.21
cat("Intercept:", round(coef(trend_model)[1], 2), 
    "| Slope:", round(coef(trend_model)[2], 2), "\n")
## Intercept: -361.58 | Slope: 14.07
cat("Bias:", round(trend_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(trend_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(trend_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(trend_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 0 | MAD: 265.31 | MSE: 92076.66 | MAPE: 107.24 %
cat("RSFE:", round(trend_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(trend_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 0 | Tracking Signal: 0
trend_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = trend_fitted
)

ggplot(trend_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Linear Trend"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Linear Trend" = "brown")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Linear Trend Projection",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

## 11. Seasonal Indices

# Monthly seasonal indices
# Extract month from time series
months <- cycle(ts_cci)
years  <- floor(time(ts_cci))

# Calculate seasonal indices
monthly_means <- tapply(as.numeric(ts_cci), months, mean)
overall_mean  <- mean(as.numeric(ts_cci))
seasonal_idx  <- monthly_means / overall_mean

cat("Seasonal Indices by Month:\n")
## Seasonal Indices by Month:
month_names <- c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                  "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec")
for(i in 1:12) {
  cat(month_names[i], ":", round(seasonal_idx[i], 4), "\n")
}
## Jan : 1.1152 
## Feb : 0.8893 
## Mar : 0.9113 
## Apr : 0.9305 
## May : 0.9439 
## Jun : 0.9599 
## Jul : 0.9993 
## Aug : 1.0181 
## Sep : 1.0307 
## Oct : 1.0485 
## Nov : 1.0611 
## Dec : 1.0818
# The series has a dominant trend; seasonal variation is minimal
# Deseasonalized values
deseasonalized <- as.numeric(ts_cci) / seasonal_idx[months]

# Seasonal indices plot
si_df <- data.frame(
  month = month_names,
  index = as.numeric(seasonal_idx)
)
si_df$month <- factor(si_df$month, levels = month_names)

ggplot(si_df, aes(x = month, y = index)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Seasonal Indices by Month",
       subtitle = "Values above 1 indicate above-average months",
       x = "Month", y = "Seasonal Index") +
  theme_minimal()

12. Additive Decomposition

# Additive decomposition
decomp_add <- decompose(ts_cci, type = "additive")

# Plot decomposition
plot(decomp_add)

# Forecast using trend + seasonal
trend_comp    <- as.numeric(decomp_add$trend)
seasonal_comp <- as.numeric(decomp_add$seasonal)
random_comp   <- as.numeric(decomp_add$random)

# Fitted values
add_fitted <- trend_comp + seasonal_comp
add_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - add_fitted
add_bias <- mean(add_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
add_mad  <- mean(abs(add_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
add_mse  <- mean(add_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
add_mape <- mean(abs(add_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
add_rsfe <- sum(add_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
add_ts   <- add_rsfe / add_mad

cat("Additive Decomposition:\n")
## Additive Decomposition:
cat("Bias:", round(add_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(add_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(add_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(add_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: -1.16 | MAD: 15.44 | MSE: 433.04 | MAPE: 4.98 %
cat("RSFE:", round(add_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(add_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: -139.79 | Tracking Signal: -9.05
add_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = add_fitted
)

ggplot(add_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Additive Decomp"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Additive Decomp" = "darkgreen")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Additive Decomposition",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

13. Multiplicative Decomposition

decomp_mult <- decompose(ts_cci, type = "multiplicative")

plot(decomp_mult)

trend_m    <- as.numeric(decomp_mult$trend)
seasonal_m <- as.numeric(decomp_mult$seasonal)

mult_fitted <- trend_m * seasonal_m
mult_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - mult_fitted
mult_bias <- mean(mult_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
mult_mad  <- mean(abs(mult_errors), na.rm = TRUE)
mult_mse  <- mean(mult_errors^2, na.rm = TRUE)
mult_mape <- mean(abs(mult_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
mult_rsfe <- sum(mult_errors, na.rm = TRUE)
mult_ts   <- mult_rsfe / mult_mad

cat("Multiplicative Decomposition:\n")
## Multiplicative Decomposition:
cat("Bias:", round(mult_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(mult_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(mult_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(mult_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: -1.6 | MAD: 8.97 | MSE: 267.66 | MAPE: 1.9 %
cat("RSFE:", round(mult_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(mult_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: -193.91 | Tracking Signal: -21.62
mult_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = mult_fitted
)

ggplot(mult_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Multiplicative Decomp"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Multiplicative Decomp" = "darkred")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Multiplicative Decomposition",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

14. Regression with Trend and Seasonal Dummies

n <- length(ts_cci)
t <- 1:n
months_factor <- as.factor(cycle(ts_cci))

reg_model <- lm(as.numeric(ts_cci) ~ t + months_factor)
summary(reg_model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = as.numeric(ts_cci) ~ t + months_factor)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -474.59 -259.78  -26.81  284.21  632.01 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     -295.7430   103.8934  -2.847   0.0052 ** 
## t                 14.0900     0.7217  19.524   <2e-16 ***
## months_factor2   -60.8531   133.0172  -0.457   0.6482    
## months_factor3   -62.1894   132.9996  -0.468   0.6409    
## months_factor4   -65.1321   132.9859  -0.490   0.6252    
## months_factor5   -71.3803   132.9761  -0.537   0.5924    
## months_factor6   -76.1839   132.9702  -0.573   0.5678    
## months_factor7   -67.3639   132.9682  -0.507   0.6134    
## months_factor8   -70.5694   132.9702  -0.531   0.5966    
## months_factor9   -77.3257   132.9761  -0.582   0.5620    
## months_factor10  -81.0676   132.9859  -0.610   0.5433    
## months_factor11  -87.7985   132.9996  -0.660   0.5104    
## months_factor12  -89.8994   133.0172  -0.676   0.5004    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 318.5 on 120 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.7616, Adjusted R-squared:  0.7377 
## F-statistic: 31.94 on 12 and 120 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
reg_fitted   <- as.numeric(fitted(reg_model))
reg_forecast <- predict(reg_model, 
                         newdata = data.frame(t = n + 1, 
                                              months_factor = factor(2, 
                                              levels = levels(months_factor))))

reg_errors <- as.numeric(ts_cci) - reg_fitted
reg_bias <- mean(reg_errors)
reg_mad  <- mean(abs(reg_errors))
reg_mse  <- mean(reg_errors^2)
reg_mape <- mean(abs(reg_errors / as.numeric(ts_cci)) * 100)
reg_rsfe <- sum(reg_errors)
reg_ts   <- reg_rsfe / reg_mad

cat("Regression with Trend+Seasonal Forecast Feb 2026:", 
    round(reg_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Regression with Trend+Seasonal Forecast Feb 2026: 1531.46
cat("Bias:", round(reg_bias, 2), "| MAD:", round(reg_mad, 2),
    "| MSE:", round(reg_mse, 2), "| MAPE:", round(reg_mape, 2), "%\n")
## Bias: 0 | MAD: 265.79 | MSE: 91552.78 | MAPE: 107.57 %
cat("RSFE:", round(reg_rsfe, 2), "| Tracking Signal:", round(reg_ts, 2), "\n")
## RSFE: 0 | Tracking Signal: 0
reg_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = reg_fitted
)

ggplot(reg_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Regression"),
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Regression" = "darkviolet")) +
  labs(title = "Actual vs Regression with Trend and Seasonal Dummies",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

## 15. Forecast Accuracy Comparison

library(knitr)

comparison <- data.frame(
  Method = c("Naive", "Moving Average (3)", "Weighted Moving Average",
             "Exponential Smoothing", "Trend-Adjusted ES (Holt)",
             "Linear Trend", "Seasonal Indices", 
             "Additive Decomposition", "Multiplicative Decomposition",
             "Regression Trend+Seasonal"),
  Bias = round(c(naive_bias, ma_bias, wma_bias, es_bias, holt_bias,
                  trend_bias, NA, add_bias, mult_bias, reg_bias), 2),
  MAD  = round(c(naive_mad, ma_mad, wma_mad, es_mad, holt_mad,
                  trend_mad, NA, add_mad, mult_mad, reg_mad), 2),
  MSE  = round(c(naive_mse, ma_mse, wma_mse, es_mse, holt_mse,
                  trend_mse, NA, add_mse, mult_mse, reg_mse), 2),
  MAPE = round(c(naive_mape, ma_mape, wma_mape, es_mape, holt_mape,
                  trend_mape, NA, add_mape, mult_mape, reg_mape), 2),
  RSFE = round(c(naive_rsfe, ma_rsfe, wma_rsfe, es_rsfe, holt_rsfe,
                  trend_rsfe, NA, add_rsfe, mult_rsfe, reg_rsfe), 2),
  Tracking_Signal = round(c(naive_ts, ma_ts, wma_ts, es_ts, holt_ts,
                              trend_ts, NA, add_ts, mult_ts, reg_ts), 2),
  Next_Period_Forecast = round(c(naive_forecast, ma_forecast, wma_forecast,
                                  es_forecast, holt_forecast, trend_forecast,
                                  NA, NA, NA, reg_forecast), 2)
)

kable(comparison, caption = "Forecast Accuracy Comparison Table")
Forecast Accuracy Comparison Table
Method Bias MAD MSE MAPE RSFE Tracking_Signal Next_Period_Forecast
Naive 16.01 16.27 1253.79 2.45 2113.11 129.87 2210.24
Moving Average (3) 15.62 15.83 889.04 2.38 2046.60 129.28 2070.19
Weighted Moving Average 7.91 8.02 251.47 1.20 1036.50 129.18 2128.53
Exponential Smoothing 17.48 17.74 1361.45 2.67 2325.33 131.05 2190.14

16. Superior Method Selection

cat("=== SUPERIOR METHOD SELECTION ===\n\n")
## === SUPERIOR METHOD SELECTION ===
cat("Selected Method: Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt's Method)\n\n")
## Selected Method: Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt's Method)
cat("Justification:\n")
## Justification:
cat("1. The CCI series shows a dominant upward trend throughout 2015-2026.\n")
## 1. The CCI series shows a dominant upward trend throughout 2015-2026.
cat("2. Holt's method explicitly models both level and trend components.\n")
## 2. Holt's method explicitly models both level and trend components.
cat("3. Alpha=0.9 gives high weight to recent observations (appropriate for\n")
## 3. Alpha=0.9 gives high weight to recent observations (appropriate for
cat("   rapidly changing inflation-driven index).\n")
##    rapidly changing inflation-driven index).
cat("4. Beta=0.3 allows moderate trend adaptation.\n")
## 4. Beta=0.3 allows moderate trend adaptation.
cat("5. MAPE is competitive and tracking signal is acceptable.\n")
## 5. MAPE is competitive and tracking signal is acceptable.
cat("6. Unlike linear trend, Holt's captures non-linear acceleration.\n\n")
## 6. Unlike linear trend, Holt's captures non-linear acceleration.
cat("Forecast for February 2026:", round(holt_forecast, 2), "\n")
## Forecast for February 2026: 2267.56
# Final plot
final_df <- data.frame(
  date   = as.numeric(time(ts_cci)),
  actual = as.numeric(ts_cci),
  fitted = holt_fitted
)

# Add forecast point
forecast_point <- data.frame(
  date   = 2026 + 1/12,
  actual = NA,
  fitted = holt_forecast
)

ggplot(final_df, aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = actual, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = fitted, color = "Holt's ES"), 
            linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(data = forecast_point, 
             aes(x = date, y = fitted), 
             color = "darkred", size = 4, shape = 17) +
  annotate("text", x = 2026.1, y = holt_forecast + 50,
           label = paste0("Feb 2026\nForecast: ", round(holt_forecast, 2)),
           color = "darkred", size = 3.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Actual" = "steelblue",
                                 "Holt's ES" = "darkred")) +
  labs(title = "Superior Method: Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing",
       subtitle = "Final Forecast for February 2026",
       x = "Year", y = "Index Value", color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2026)

17. Final Forecast Summary

final_result <- data.frame(
  Item  = c("Selected Superior Method", "Data Access Date",
             "Latest Available Observation", "Forecast Target Period",
             "Forecasted Value", "Alpha", "Beta"),
  Value = c("Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt's Method)",
             format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d"),
             "January 2026", "February 2026",
             round(holt_forecast, 2), "0.9", "0.3")
)

kable(final_result, caption = "Final Forecast Result")
Final Forecast Result
Item Value
Selected Superior Method Trend-Adjusted Exponential Smoothing (Holt’s Method)
Data Access Date 2026-05-28
Latest Available Observation January 2026
Forecast Target Period February 2026
Forecasted Value 2267.56
Alpha 0.9
Beta 0.3 
cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
## 
## Interpretation:
cat("The Construction Cost Index is forecast to reach", 
    round(holt_forecast, 2), "in February 2026.\n")
## The Construction Cost Index is forecast to reach 2267.56 in February 2026.
cat("This represents a continued upward trend driven by inflation\n")
## This represents a continued upward trend driven by inflation
cat("and rising construction material costs in Turkey.\n")
## and rising construction material costs in Turkey.

18. Limitations

The following limitations should be considered when interpreting this forecast:

  • The series contains a structural break around 2021-2022 due to Turkey’s high inflation period, which may reduce forecast accuracy.
  • Seasonal patterns are weak compared to the trend, so seasonal methods add limited value.
  • Only 133 monthly observations are available (2015-2026), which limits the robustness of decomposition methods.
  • The forecast is for one period ahead only; longer-horizon forecasts would carry much higher uncertainty.
  • External shocks (policy changes, commodity price spikes) cannot be captured by time series methods alone.

19. Reproducibility

This project can be reproduced by:

  1. Installing R 4.6.0 and RStudio
  2. Installing the tuikr package from GitHub: pak::pak("emraher/tuikr")
  3. Installing required packages: httr, readxl, dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, scales, forecast
  4. Opening forecasting_project.Rmd and clicking Knit
  5. Data is accessed directly from TÜİK via tuikr::statistical_tables() — no manual download required
